We used to answer the phones live five days a week, normal hours, right? And my mother was the receptionist and she died and didn't have two of them, only had one. So temporarily, you know, we're like covering, so we went to three days a week. And I think short hours on those three days. And then people took turns, right? You know, I was a little slow to start looking for a replacement and running ads. And then I'm trying to find out how is it affecting us? Well, other than the three people who are covering being grumpy, it isn't affecting us at all. It's not having any impact at all on the business. So I'm thinking, why do I want a full-time, you know. So then we went to two days, then we went to one day, now we're at no days. Now the product stuff has moved, I wouldn't be doing it if I had product sales still. So now, for me, we're at no days. You know, she never answers. And does it make any difference? No. But again, it is what they expect, right? And so like I do business, and I would be prone to do business with your clients where I you, I do business entirely by phone appointment. So somebody's got to request it, have it pre-arranged. And like in my case, I'm blocking about a day and a half a month when all my phone appointments get scheduled in a clump so I can get it all, you know. So like the day, if you guys are Go Plus members, which I assume everybody is, the day we do the Go Plus call, that's a day in phone day. I've had phone appointments since seven o'clock in the morning all day long and they may be clients we're working with, they may be new prospects, they may be whatever, and Vicki clumps them all into one day. And so I have like my one day of misery and then I don't have to talk to anybody for like two weeks, right? And so if it's two weeks away and they got to have a phone appointment, they fit into the next available day on which there's a phone appointment. The way that is, if you will, sold to people is the obvious benefit is no phone tag, no me chasing you, you chasing me, so no frustration about that, no uncertainty, and you got my undivided attention for the amount of time that we got blocked for your phone appointment. You know what the deal is, I know what the deal is, you're organized, I'm organized, you got me, right? So even people for whom this is very strange, they get it and they adapt to it. It's mainly about explaining it to them, you know, to start with. And if you have a client base now where they, where you feel like, they're going to feel like something's being taken away from them, then at the same time give them something new that is not painful to you. The monthly client tell us seminar group tell us it, so give them some new benefit. At the same time you change the rules and they feel like there's something being taken away from them. But see, so my option would be like your option, my other option would be to attempt to deliver the other kind of service and deliver it badly, because I'm not willing to staff to deliver it well. So rather than doing that, I'd rather create something I can deliver and then train them to accept that. So now they're getting exactly what they were promised. I mean precisely. I'm Southwest. Mike. One of the things we talked about was no free lunch and some different things like that. I'm using the most incredible free gift ever, of course it's an IVA and I use it even in my niche market, where I give them three months for free of our newsletter and some different pieces of that. How do we balance that out with staying, you know, the guru? Well, first of all, it's product versus manual labor. That's one thing. Second of all, it really isn't free, as you very well know. I believe at the moment it's $19.95, it's just free because we say it's free. So there is a little skin in the game, but if you will, it's your low end point of entry. Now, so let's translate it to something that would be a very bad idea. That would be three free months of attending chapter meetings without ever joining. It would be come to three coaching meetings and then make up your mind whether you're going to join. And the worst thing is a lot of free idea meetings. The advertising industry revolves around free pitches.
